epics.title: Markdown Live Preview - Inline Formatting
epics.description: Render inline markdown formatting (bold, italic, code, links, headers, strikethrough) visually in the editor while keeping the buffer as raw markdown. Markers are hidden unless the cursor is on the affected line, similar to Obsidian's live preview mode.

user_stories.title: Markdown mode auto-detection and toggle
user_stories.description: Automatically enable markdown live preview mode when opening .md or .markdown files. Add a toggle in the View menu and a keyboard shortcut to enable/disable the preview rendering. When disabled, the file renders as plain text with syntax highlighting.
acceptance_criteria: Given a file with .md extension, when opened in the editor, then markdown live preview mode is automatically enabled
Given a file with .markdown extension, when opened, then markdown mode is enabled
Given a .go or .py file, when opened, then markdown mode is not enabled
Given the View menu, when 'Markdown Preview' is selected, then the mode toggles on or off for the current tab
Given markdown mode is active, when the status bar renders, then it shows 'MD' indicator
Given markdown mode is disabled via toggle, when the file renders, then raw markdown markers are visible as plain text

user_stories.title: Bold and italic rendering with marker hiding
user_stories.description: Render **bold** text in bold style and *italic* text in italic style. Hide the marker characters (** and *) unless the cursor is on the same line as the markers. Support nested bold+italic (***text***).
acceptance_criteria: Given '**hello**' in the buffer, when the cursor is on a different line, then 'hello' renders in bold and the ** markers are hidden
Given '*world*' in the buffer, when the cursor is on a different line, then 'world' renders in italic and the * markers are hidden
Given '***both***' in the buffer, when the cursor is on a different line, then 'both' renders in bold+italic and markers are hidden
Given '**hello**' and the cursor is on that line, when rendering, then the ** markers are visible and the text between them is still bold
Given the cursor moves away from a line with markers, when the line re-renders, then markers disappear and text shifts left to fill the gap
Given 'no **markers here' (unmatched), when rendering, then the text displays as-is with visible **
Given bold text in the middle of a line like 'foo **bar** baz', when rendered with cursor elsewhere, then 'foo bar baz' shows with 'bar' in bold and correct spacing
Given cursor column calculations, when clicking on rendered text with hidden markers, then the cursor maps to the correct buffer column

user_stories.title: Header rendering with level-based styling
user_stories.description: Render markdown headers (# through ######) with distinct visual styles: larger/bolder for h1, progressively less prominent for lower levels. Hide the # prefix and space unless the cursor is on that line.
acceptance_criteria: Given '# Title' on a line, when cursor is elsewhere, then 'Title' renders in bold with h1 color and the '# ' prefix is hidden
Given '## Subtitle', when cursor is elsewhere, then 'Subtitle' renders in bold with h2 color and '## ' is hidden
Given '### Section', when cursor is elsewhere, then 'Section' renders with h3 styling
Given '# Title' and cursor is on that line, when rendering, then '# Title' shows in full with the # visible and the text styled
Given a line starting with '#' but not followed by a space (like '#hashtag'), when rendering, then it is not treated as a header
Given headers h1 through h6, when rendered, then each level has a visually distinct color or intensity

user_stories.title: Inline code span rendering
user_stories.description: Render `code` spans with a distinct background color and monospace appearance. Hide the backtick markers unless the cursor is on that line.
acceptance_criteria: Given '`code`' in text, when cursor is on a different line, then 'code' renders with a tinted background and the backticks are hidden
Given '`code`' and cursor is on that line, when rendering, then backticks are visible and the text between them has the tinted background
Given 'use `foo` and `bar`' with multiple code spans, when rendered, then both spans are independently styled
Given '``literal backtick``' (double backtick escape), when rendered, then it handles the double-backtick syntax correctly
Given an unmatched single backtick, when rendering, then it displays as a literal backtick

user_stories.title: Link rendering with URL hiding
user_stories.description: Render [text](url) links showing only the link text in a link color. The full markdown syntax is revealed when the cursor is on that line. Also handle ![alt](url) image references.
acceptance_criteria: Given '[Click here](https://example.com)' in text, when cursor is elsewhere, then 'Click here' renders in link color (blue/cyan) and the URL portion is hidden
Given the cursor is on the link line, when rendering, then the full '[Click here](https://example.com)' is visible with link text colored
Given '![alt text](image.png)', when cursor is elsewhere, then 'alt text' renders with an image indicator prefix
Given a bare URL like 'https://example.com' (not in [] syntax), when rendering, then it shows as-is in link color
Given '[text](url) more text', when rendered with hidden URL, then 'more text' follows immediately after 'text' with correct spacing

user_stories.title: Strikethrough and horizontal rule rendering
user_stories.description: Render ~~strikethrough~~ text with a dim or crossed style. Render --- or *** or ___ horizontal rules as a full-width line.
acceptance_criteria: Given '~~deleted~~' in text, when cursor is elsewhere, then 'deleted' renders in dim/gray style and the ~~ markers are hidden
Given '~~deleted~~' and cursor is on that line, when rendering, then ~~ markers are visible
Given '---' on a line by itself, when rendered, then a horizontal line spans the editor width
Given '***' on a line by itself, when rendered, then a horizontal line is drawn (same as ---)
Given '---' as part of a YAML frontmatter block at the top of the file, when rendering, then it is not converted to a horizontal rule

user_stories.title: Cursor column mapping for hidden markers
user_stories.description: When markers are hidden (cursor on a different line), the visible text shifts left. Mouse clicks and cursor positioning must map correctly between visible screen columns and actual buffer columns. This is the core infrastructure that makes all marker hiding work correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given '**bold** rest' with hidden markers, when clicking on the 'r' in 'rest', then the cursor is placed at the correct buffer column (after the closing **)
Given 'before **bold** after' with markers hidden, when pressing End, then the cursor goes to the true end of the buffer line
Given 'before **bold** after' with markers hidden, when pressing Home, then the cursor goes to column 0
Given a line with multiple hidden marker pairs, when navigating with arrow keys, then cursor movement corresponds to visible character positions
Given the cursor moves to a line with hidden markers, when the line becomes the cursor line, then markers appear and the cursor position adjusts to show the correct character
Given word wrap is enabled, when a line with hidden markers wraps, then the wrap point is calculated based on visible width (excluding hidden markers)

epics.title: Markdown Live Preview - Block Elements
epics.description: Render block-level markdown elements (code blocks, blockquotes, lists, tables) with visual styling while keeping the buffer as raw markdown. Block markers are hidden or transformed unless the cursor is inside the block.

user_stories.title: Fenced code block rendering
user_stories.description: Render ```language fenced code blocks with a tinted background spanning the full width. Apply syntax highlighting inside the block using Chroma (reusing the existing highlight engine). Hide the ``` fence lines unless the cursor is inside the block.
acceptance_criteria: Given a fenced block with ```python, when cursor is outside the block, then the ``` lines are hidden and the code renders with a tinted background
Given a fenced block with ```go, when rendered, then Go syntax highlighting is applied to the code inside the block
Given the cursor is inside a fenced block, when rendering, then the ``` fence lines are visible
Given a fenced block, when rendered, then the background color extends to the full editor width for all lines in the block
Given a fenced block without a language tag (just ```), when rendered, then code renders with tinted background but no syntax highlighting
Given nested backticks inside a fenced block, when rendering, then they are not treated as code span markers

user_stories.title: Blockquote rendering
user_stories.description: Render > blockquote lines with a colored left border bar and slightly indented text. The > character is replaced with a visual border. Nested blockquotes (>>) show deeper indentation.
acceptance_criteria: Given '> quoted text', when cursor is elsewhere, then a colored vertical bar appears at the left and 'quoted text' is indented, with '> ' hidden
Given '> quoted text' and cursor is on that line, when rendering, then '> quoted text' shows in full with the > visible
Given '>> nested quote', when rendered with cursor elsewhere, then two vertical bars appear showing nesting depth
Given multiple consecutive > lines, when rendered, then the vertical bar is continuous across all lines
Given a blockquote with bold text like '> **important**', when rendered, then both blockquote styling and bold apply

user_stories.title: List rendering with bullet transformation
user_stories.description: Render unordered list markers (-, *, +) as a bullet character. Render ordered lists (1., 2.) as-is. Render checkbox lists (- [ ] and - [x]) with checkbox characters. Preserve indentation for nested lists.
acceptance_criteria: Given '- item' in the buffer, when cursor is elsewhere, then the '-' is replaced with a bullet character and the text is properly spaced
Given '- item' and cursor is on that line, when rendering, then the raw '- item' is shown
Given '  - nested', when rendered, then indentation is preserved with a bullet at the nested level
Given '1. first', when rendered, then it displays as-is (numbered lists don't need transformation)
Given '- [ ] todo', when cursor is elsewhere, then an empty checkbox character replaces '- [ ]'
Given '- [x] done', when cursor is elsewhere, then a checked checkbox character replaces '- [x]'
Given a mix of list levels, when rendered, then indentation hierarchy is visually clear

user_stories.title: Table rendering with aligned columns
user_stories.description: Render markdown tables with aligned columns and box-drawing borders. Calculate column widths from the widest cell in each column. When the cursor is inside a table, show raw pipe characters. When outside, render with clean borders and padding.
acceptance_criteria: Given a markdown table with | col1 | col2 |, when cursor is outside the table, then columns are aligned with consistent width and box-drawing borders replace the | characters
Given a table header row followed by |---|---|, when rendered, then the separator row becomes a horizontal border line
Given cells with varying content lengths, when rendered, then all cells in a column are padded to the widest cell's width
Given the cursor is inside the table, when rendering, then raw | characters and content are shown for editing
Given a table with alignment markers (|:---|, |:---:|, |---:|), when rendered, then cell content is left/center/right aligned accordingly
Given cursor moves out of the table, when re-rendering, then the table snaps to aligned view
Given a table with 5 columns and 10 rows, when rendered, then all columns align correctly across all rows
Given editing a cell to make it wider than the column, when cursor moves out, then the column width recalculates to fit

user_stories.title: YAML frontmatter handling
user_stories.description: Detect YAML frontmatter (--- delimited block at the start of the file) and render it with a distinct background or collapsed view. Don't treat the --- delimiters as horizontal rules.
acceptance_criteria: Given a file starting with '---\ntitle: Hello\n---', when rendered, then the frontmatter block has a distinct tinted background
Given frontmatter at the top of the file, when rendering, then the '---' lines are not converted to horizontal rules
Given frontmatter content, when rendered, then YAML syntax highlighting is applied inside the block
Given no frontmatter (file doesn't start with ---), when rendering, then normal markdown rendering applies from line 1
Given the cursor is inside the frontmatter block, when rendering, then raw content is fully visible for editing
